2604.23732.title: Impact of Age Specialized Models for Hypoglycemia Classification
2604.23732.paper_summary: Disease progression varies with age and is influenced by underlying genetic, biochemical, and hormonal etiologies, suggesting the need for tailored monitoring, care, and medication beyond standard clinical guidelines. Specifically, in autoimmune diseases like type 1 diabetes (T1D), where patients depend on exogenous insulin to compensate for insulin deficiency, medication dosing and the physiological response reflected in vital signs can differ. Insulin therapy can lead to hypoglycemia, a dangerous condition characterized by decreased blood glucose levels ($\leq$70). This risk can be mitigated through improved diabetes management supported by data analytics. Notably, leveraging data from continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) devices, hypoglycemia onset can be predicted. However, while glucose variability, auto-antibody levels, and hypoglycemia occurrence differ across age groups, hypoglycemia classification most often only relies on population-based models specialized in specific age ranges. In this work, we classify hypoglycemia 0, 5-15, 20-45, and 50-120 minutes before onset using DiaData, a large CGM dataset of patients with T1D ranging from children to seniors. In particular, we investigate: 1) the generalizability of a population-based model including all age groups, 2) the impact of age-segmented models trained separately per age group, and 3) the effect of model individualization through transfer learning. The results show that a global population-based model yields similar or superior performance compared to age-segmented models. These findings suggest that data from children, teenagers, and adults can be combined for training models on hypoglycemia classification. While glucose variation differs across age groups, short-term hypoglycemic patterns are similar. However, data of children obtain their best recall with age specialized model.
2604.23732.paper_summary_zh: 疾病進展因年齡而異，並受到潛在的遺傳、生化和荷爾蒙病因的影響，這表明需要超越標準臨床指南的量身定制監測、護理和用藥。具體而言，在自體免疫疾病如1型糖尿病（T1D）中，患者依賴外源性胰島素來補償胰島素缺乏，藥物劑量和反映在生命體徵中的生理反應可能會有所不同。胰島素療法可能導致低血糖，這是一種以血糖水平降低（$\leq$70）為特徵的危險狀況。這一風險可以通過數據分析支持的改善糖尿病管理來減輕。值得注意的是，利用持續血糖監測（CGM）設備的數據，可以預測低血糖的發作。然而，儘管血糖變異性、自體抗體水平和低血糖發生率在不同年齡組之間有所不同，低血糖的分類通常僅依賴於專注於特定年齡範圍的人群模型。在這項工作中，我們使用DiaData，一個涵蓋從兒童到老年人的T1D患者的大型CGM數據集，將低血糖分為發作前0、5-15、20-45和50-120分鐘。特別地，我們調查：1）包括所有年齡組的人群模型的普遍性，2）按年齡組別分別訓練的年齡分段模型的影響，以及3）通過轉移學習實現模型個性化的效果。結果顯示，全球性的人群模型相比於年齡分段模型產生了相似或更優的表現。這些發現表明，來自兒童、青少年和成人的數據可以結合起來用於低血糖分類模型的訓練。雖然血糖變化在不同年齡組之間有所不同，但短期低血糖模式是相似的。然而，兒童的數據在專注於年齡的模型中獲得最佳的召回率。
2604.23732.author: Beyza Cinar et.al.
